[[BLOCK:p_0]]
Lecturer Profile

[[BLOCK:p_1]]
Gong Wen

[[BLOCK:p_2]]
Ph.D. in Psychology (1997, Psychometrics (心理测量学)), Beijing Normal University

[[BLOCK:p_3]]
Associate Professor, School of Business, Beijing Technology and Business University

[[BLOCK:p_5]]
Background: In the early 1990s, founded one of China's earliest consulting firms specializing in talent assessment, introducing Western theories and tools for talent evaluation to support domestic talent selection. Joined Founder Group in 2000 and led the development of China's first networked psychological assessment tool, the "Peking University Founder Professional Assessment System (北大方正专业评估系统)," in 2002. Over the past decade, this product has remained the market leader in China for college entrance guidance tools and was the first profitable online psychological assessment tool in the country. Returned to academia in 2005, balancing teaching responsibilities with roles as a corporate management consultant, external lecturer, and executive coach.

[[BLOCK:p_7]]
Areas of Expertise: Dedicated to applying Positive Psychology (积极心理学) theories to corporate management, particularly in human resource management practices. Possesses over 30 years of hands-on experience in talent assessment and selection, learning and development, and leadership development. Published more than 20 papers in core journals such as "Peking University Business Review (北大商业评论)," "China Foreign Management," "China Human Resource Development," "Future and Development," and "Enterprise Management," with several articles indexed by CSSCI.

[[BLOCK:p_9]]
University Courses: Teaches undergraduate, academic postgraduate, and MBA courses, including "Management," "Organizational Behavior," "Human Resource Management," "Self-Awareness and Self-Management," "Selected Readings from Zizhi Tongjian," and "Leadership and Team Empowerment (领导力与团队赋能)."
